data.title: Canadian Dollar Is the Most Heavily Shorted Currency as Trump Escalates Trade War
data.description:
Canada's dollar has become the most heavily shorted currency as U.S. President Donald Trump escalates his trade war against America's northern neighbor.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Speculators have raised their bearish bets against the Canadian dollar to the highest level among major currencies as U.S. President Donald Trump announced new tariffs on Canada of up to 50% on some goods, helping to push the Canadian currency to ‌a 14-month low.

Short sellers have wagered $12.5 billion that the Canadian dollar has further to fall, according to data from the ‌U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Trump's proposed 50% tariffs on a wide range of goods are scheduled to take effect on Aug. 19 of this year.

Worse Than the Japanese Yen

Short sellers have ratcheted up their bets against Canada's dollar for two consecutive weeks amid signs of continued economic weakness. Earlier this year, Canada's economy entered a recession defined as two consecutive quarters of contraction. Canada's dollar is now the most shorted currency traded on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME).

Canada's dollar has overtaken the Japanese yen as the most widely shorted currency globally. And the Japanese yen fell to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar in 40 years at the end of June. The decline in the Canadian dollar has accelerated since Trump declined to ⁠extend the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement on trade, starting a decade-long wind down of the free trade deal.

Is LULU Stock a Buy?

Let's look at the stock of Lululemon Athletica (LULU), one of Canada's best-known companies. LULU shares have a consensus Hold rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy and 20 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LULU price target of $167.38 implies 34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Ramps to 18-Month High as Oil Soars
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield rose 5 basis points to 4.707% on Wednesday, marking its highest level since January 2025.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Rising inflation expectations caused by higher oil prices are behind the uptick. Brent crude (BZ), the global oil benchmark, is up by over 6% on the day after the Iran-backed Houthi military group attacked two Saudi tankers attempting to cross the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. The Houthis had previously imposed a maritime blockade on Saudi ports, further threatening global energy prices as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains restricted.

Inflation Risks Keep Fed Hike Bets Alive

Higher inflation expectations could push the Fed to keep rates higher for longer. That can drive yields higher as investors adjust to a more hawkish outlook and demand additional compensation for holding government debt.

The Fed's next interest rate decision is scheduled for July 29. The central bank will likely hold rates steady, with the CME FedWatch tool assigning 62.1% odds to the scenario. However, the odds of a 25 bps rate hike have been steadily rising and are at 37.9%, up from 34.2% a day ago and 11.8% a week ago.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: M&A News: Canada's Brookfield (BN) Buys Battery Storage Firm Aypa Power for $7 Billion
data.description:
Canadian investment management firm Brookfield Corp. (BN) says it is buying privately held battery storage company Aypa Power for $7 billion.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Brookfield is acquiring Aypa Power from Blackstone Energy Transition Partners. Aypa Power is the largest standalone battery storage developer in North America with a diversified portfolio across both the U.S. and Canada.

Under terms of the deal, Brookfield will buy Aypa Power's project portfolio, its development platform, and absorb its team of about 200 employees. The acquisition is subject to regulatory approvals in Canada and the United States.

Energy Growth and Performance

Brookfield said in a news release announcing the deal that battery storage is increasingly important in today's energy market. The growth of battery-powered vehicles, as well as rising demand for energy among artificial intelligence (AI) data centers, has put energy companies front-and-center.

Brookfield manages money for large institutions, insurance companies, and individual investors. It earns fees for managing that capital. Brookfield pays out more than 90% of its earnings to shareholders via dividends. BN stock has a forward dividend yield of about 4%.

Is BN Stock a Buy?

The stock of Brookfield Corp. has a consensus Strong Buy rating among six Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on six Buy ratings issued in the last three months. The average BN price target of $56.17 implies 36% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) Stock Soars on a Leadership Change and Earnings Beat
data.description:
Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) stock rallied over 18% on Thursday after the steel manufacturing company announced a leadership change alongside its latest earnings report. The company promoted Chief Financial Officer Celso Goncalves to president while keeping his role as CFO. It also appointed Goncalves to its board of directors, effective immediately. Chairman and CEO Lourenco Goncalves will remain in charge of the company but will give up the title of president as part of the leadership change.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Celso Goncalves has served as CFO since 2021 and joined Cleveland-Cliffs in 2016. The company credited him with helping improve its financial position and supporting key acquisitions that turned Cleveland-Cliffs into North America's largest flat-rolled steel producer.

"This appointment also represents the beginning of a thoughtful and deliberate leadership transition toward the future of Cleveland-Cliffs," said Cleveland-Cliffs CEO Lourenco Goncalves. He added that he plans to continue leading the company for years to come while Celso's expanded role helps provide long-term leadership continuity.

Cleveland-Cliffs Beats Wall Street's Q2 Estimates

Cleveland-Cliffs announced the leadership change at the same time it reported its Q2 2026 results. The company posted adjusted earnings per share of -20 cents alongside revenue of $5.23 billion. Both of these results were better than Wall Street's estimates of -21 cents per share and revenue of $5.15 billion.

Cleveland-Cliffs said that it expects the second half of 2026 to be better than the first half of the year. On top of that, the company expects its Q4 adjusted EBITDA to outperform Q3 and see further gains in 2027. Cleveland-Cliffs also told investors that reducing its debt is the company's number one priority.

Is Cleveland-Cliffs Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Cleveland-Cliffs is Hold, based on seven Buy and one Sell ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average CLF stock price target of $10.88, suggesting a possible 3.06% downside for the shares. These ratings and price targets will likely change as analysts update their coverage after today's earnings report. (See CLF Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: 'Here it Comes,' Says Top Investor About Palantir Stock
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) fell once more this week, pressured by worries that an open-source alternative could spell the end of its AI-powered gravy train.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

However, that's not been the only overhang on its share price, which has fallen roughly 30% year-to-date. The company's elevated valuation has likely been scaring off would-be investors, who might otherwise be enticed by its stunning growth.

Indeed, Palantir hasn't exactly been a slouch when it comes to both top- and bottom-line growth. Its Rule of 40 score of 145% in Q1 2026 leaves little room to doubt its past performance.

Still, as has been the case this year, even stunning results haven't been enough to ward off the bears. As the company gears up to deliver its Q2 2026 earnings report on August 3rd after the markets close, could this time be different?

Top investor Harsh Chauhan is counting on it.

"Palantir's potential beat-and-raise quarterly report could give the stock a big boost," declares the 5-star investor, who is among the top 1% of stock pros covered by TipRanks and a writer for The Motley Fool.

Chauhan notes that things have been improving, with investor sentiment starting to turn. He points to the positive movement of the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF over the past few months as an indication that things are getting better.

Palantir also has a tendency to surpass Wall Street expectations, notes Chauhan, and he is fully expecting this to happen once more. He cites the company's growing contract value, which rose by 61% last quarter to reach $2.41 billion, as concrete evidence of its growing popularity.

Once again, Chauhan argues, it's shaping up to be another strong quarter. Except now he thinks the wind just might be at Palantir's back.

"This time, the market could reward Palantir for its improving growth trajectory," Chauhan surmises.

Moreover, the investor cites estimates that the AI software market is expected to continue growing at a fast clip between 2024 and 2034. That could boost Palantir's addressable opportunity even further, he adds.

In other words, get ready for some positive moves.

"The software specialist may even see a parabolic move in its stock price," he concludes. (To watch Chauhan's track record, click here)

Wall Street is feeling moderately confident about PLTR. With 15 Buys, 4 Holds, and 2 Sells, PLTR enjoys a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $182.71 points to gains approaching 50%. (See PLTR stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: U.S. Initial Jobless Claims Plunge to 57-Year Low
data.description:
U.S. initial jobless claims are at their lowest level since 1969 as the labor market continues to hold strong. That comes despite fears of AI-driven job displacement and rising tensions in the Middle East.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

For the week ended July 18, initial jobless claims fell by 22,000 to 187,000, below the estimate of 210,000.  Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, declined by 2,000 to 1.796 million, also trailing the estimate of 1.809 million. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing jobless claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

Labor Market Holds Up as Fed Eyes Inflation

The data shows that the U.S. labor market remains in a slow-to-fire-and-hire environment. At the same time, one factor that could explain the low initial claims data is a weakening participation rate. Last month, the rate fell to 61.5%, the lowest level in over five years. The rate measures the share of the working-age population that is either employed or actively looking for work.

Inflation remains the key focus within the Fed's dual mandate as the labor market holds up. The central bank is scheduled to hold its next interest rate decision on July 29, where it is likely to hold rates steady, according to the CME FedWatch tool.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Last Minute-Thought: Goldman Sachs Weighs In on Intel Stock Ahead of Q2 Earnings
data.description:
Investors have really bought into Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) turnaround story, making the chip giant one of 2026's biggest success stories. Even after the recent pullback, the shares still show year-to-date gains of 181%.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Nevertheless, the market appears to have gotten nervous about high-flying chip stocks, with the selloff triggered by a mix of elevated valuations, profit-taking and questions about whether the current pace of AI infrastructure investment can be sustained over the long term.

However, heading into today's Q2 earnings release, Goldman Sachs' James Schneider, an analyst ranked among the top 4% on Wall Street, sees a "constructive setup" for the stock.

The company continues to reach important milestones in its foundry business, while customer engagements are progressing in a positive way. Schneider also sees a favorable backdrop for Intel's server CPU business, with demand benefiting from the emergence of agentic AI. "Overall," the 5-star analyst added, "while we could see some near-term transitory headwinds, we believe the stock continues to trade on a structural turnaround thesis which we expect to stay intact post the print."

As for the results, Schneider expects a solid quarter and guide. The analyst sees "modest upside" to the Q2 results, while the Q3 guide is expected to be broadly in line with consensus. The analyst thinks strength in server CPUs will be partly offset by softer PC trends.

Key areas of investor attention are likely to include Intel's progress with external foundry customers, and the potential upside from advanced packaging. For the former, Schneider expects the company to provide additional details on external customer announcements for its foundry business, which are anticipated in the second half of 2026. As for the latter, there should be an update on advanced packaging opportunities amid continued strength in AI-related spending.

Server CPU demand is another potential catalyst, with agentic AI supporting solid demand and potentially providing further near-term upside to revenue. Investors are also likely to seek greater clarity on Intel's market-share outlook and supply availability.

Lastly, gross margins will also remain an important area of focus. With Intel continuing to ramp its 18A and 14A process nodes, Schneider expects investors to pay close attention to the factors that could pressure or support gross margins during the second half of the year.

Bottom line, Schneider isn't quite ready to recommend buying INTC shares, keeping a Neutral rating. Still, his $150 price target points to 45% upside over the next 12 months. (To watch Schneider's track record, click here)

The Street's average price target is a more modest $114.93, a figure implying 12-month share gains of 11%. On the rating front, based on 22 Holds, 10 Buys and 2 Sells, the analyst consensus views INTC as a Hold. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Elon Musk Warned SpaceX Short Sellers — But Their Gains Just Hit $15.5 Billion
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock fell over 2% on Thursday, extending a sharp drop that has pushed the shares below its $135 IPO price. The decline has handed short sellers an estimated $15.5 billion in paper gains through July 22, according to Ortex data cited by Reuters. That comes despite CEO Elon Musk's recent warning that firms with large short positions in SpaceX have a "very low" chance of surviving over time.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

Despite Musk's warning, short sellers have continued betting against SpaceX as the stock has fallen.

Short Sellers Keep Betting Against SpaceX

Short selling is a bet that a stock will fall. A short seller borrows shares and sells them, hoping to buy them back later at a lower price and pocket the difference.

Ortex estimates that nearly 196 million SpaceX shares were sold short in late July, or about 31% of the shares available for trading. That is up sharply from roughly 40 million shares around the time of the IPO. Those bets have paid off so far. Short sellers' estimated paper gains stood at $8.7 billion on July 16. By July 22, that figure had climbed to $15.5 billion.

SpaceX went public at $135 per share on June 11 and surged as high as $225.64 soon after. The stock has since dropped to around $114, wiping out much of its early rally.

Why the Short Trade Could Still Be Risky

The growing short position cuts both ways. If SpaceX keeps falling, short sellers could add to their gains. But with so many shares now sold short, a sudden rally could quickly turn against them.

If SPCX rises sharply, some short sellers may rush to buy shares to close their positions and limit losses. That buying could push the stock even higher, creating what is known as a short squeeze.

This helps explain Musk's warning. He believes SpaceX's long-term growth could eventually prove short sellers wrong. For now, though, the stock's slide has worked firmly in their favor.

SpaceX Earnings and Share Unlock Come Next

SpaceX is set to report its first quarterly results as a public company on August 4. Investors will be watching for signs of growth and updates on the company's spending plans.

Then comes another major event. Starting August 6, about 911.5 million shares held by insiders and other early holders could become eligible for sale. That does not mean all those shares will be sold. However, if many holders decide to sell, the added supply could put more pressure on SPCX stock.

With earnings and the share unlock approaching, short sellers now face a big test. The next major move in SPCX could decide whether their $15.5 billion in gains grow — or start to disappear.

What Is SPCX's Stock Price Target?

SPCX stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks based on 23 Buys, five Holds, and one Sell assigned after its IPO. The average price target for SpaceX is $243.81, implying an upside of 115.74% from the current price.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
